title: Published Codeberg-first Claude Code comparison page in repo docs
chosen_action: Create a high-intent comparison page for evaluators searching Claude Code alternatives and route them into the Codeberg-first first-task path.
why_this_action: Fresh audit says same-family directory and curator bursts should pause while measurement windows are still open.
Adoption funnel says the current bottleneck is conversion from interest to free use.
Shared competitor findings already identified Claude Code as a major comparison surface.
A Codeberg-first comparison page improves evaluator conversion without wasting another same-day outreach cycle.
expected_outcome: Higher conversion quality from comparison-intent traffic into START_HERE, first-task evaluation, and Codeberg-primary repo inspection.

measurement_window.success_signal: Any evidence that comparison-intent surfaces are being reused in distribution or referenced from future outreach plus a positive Codeberg star/watch/fork delta within the broader adoption window.
measurement_window.failure_signal: No reuse in follow-on distribution and no Codeberg adoption movement attributable to stronger conversion surfaces.

replacement_if_fails: Promote a different bottom-funnel asset next: a public 'Ralph Workflow vs Cursor' or 'How to tell if an AI coding task is actually done' comparison/guide, then pair it with a fresh non-overlapping distribution lane.

verification.method: Local path validation for all new internal links

docs_review_note.what_changed: Added a new comparison page and linked it from docs/README.md under a new Comparisons section.
docs_review_note.why_here: The page is a high-intent evaluator asset; docs/README is the right discovery surface without bloating the top-level README route list.
docs_review_note.what_was_pruned_or_why_not: Nothing was removed because the top-level surfaces were already short; keeping README unchanged avoids turning it into a link farm.
docs_review_note.duplication_reduced: The new page reuses existing first-task, review, and workflow guides instead of restating all of that material in multiple top-level surfaces.
docs_review_note.why_top_level_is_better: Evaluators who arrive with Claude Code context now have a direct comparison path without making the first screen noisier for everyone else.
